serverNeeds.description: Prior-period dated trend series for the N4 dashed comparison overlay. Either extend fetchPerformanceTrend with an offset/startDate window, or ship client-side by fetching 2×days and splitting into halves (zero server change).
serverNeeds.evidence: search-comparison returns aggregate totals only (SearchComparison, shared/types/analytics.ts:47-52); performance-trend accepts only `days` ending today (server/routes/google.ts:258-270) — the doc's 'comparison data already exists' claim is wrong for a per-date series.
serverNeeds.whereItLives: server/routes/google.ts + the fetchPerformanceTrend helper (or client-side split in src/hooks/admin/useAdminSearch.ts)

serverNeeds.description: Cross-workspace GSC roll-up endpoint (aggregate clicks/traffic/position across all workspaces + per-client rows) for the N1 'Across your book' scope. Only needed if N1 is adopted.
serverNeeds.evidence: grep 'cross-workspace|allWorkspaces|aggregate' in server/routes/google.ts came up empty; every GSC endpoint is per-site behind requireWorkspaceSiteAccessFromQuery (google.ts:245-322).
serverNeeds.whereItLives: new module, e.g. server/domains/analytics/book-rollup.ts + route in server/routes/google.ts or a global-ops route

serverNeeds.description: Wins→proof-points write contract for the N3 graduation bridge (stage a search win into the Insights Engine as a proof point). Only needed if N3 is adopted.
serverNeeds.evidence: grep 'proof_point|proofPoint' across server/, shared/, src/ returned zero code hits; the only mentions are 'proposed'-class lexicon entries describing mockup concepts (shared/types/lexicon.ts:497,521).
serverNeeds.whereItLives: server/domains (insights/outcomes seam) + shared/types contract; cross-surface with Insights Engine

openQuestions.question: OQ1: Overview tab — is the unified GSC+GA4 view (merged chart, cross-source toggle cards, feed) cut in favor of narrative headers, or does the rebuild keep a 4th sub-view? (O1-O4)
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep Overview as a 4th lens — data merge already exists in useAnalyticsOverview; cutting it is a capability deletion without a named home (hard floor).

openQuestions.question: OQ2: Insight feed home — inside Search & Traffic (pills, chips, row badges) or exclusively in the Insights Engine? (I1-I4, Q6, Q12, G4)
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Reuse the live InsightFeed component unstyled inside the rebuilt tabs (doc's recommended bridge); restyle in a later pass.

openQuestions.question: OQ3: Diagnostics — keep the Run Deep Diagnostic CTA + ?report= deep link (destination page still exists at HEAD), or cut deliberately? The ledger's only Gap.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep CTA pointing at the existing diagnostics page unchanged; re-home the page as its own decision.

openQuestions.question: OQ4: Sign off scope + data contracts for N1 book roll-up, N2 rank movers, N3 graduation bridge before build.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Defer N1/N3 (need sn-2/sn-3); N2 ships as link-out to Keyword Hub, embedded panel later.

openQuestions.question: OQ5: Dormant admin GSC chat endpoint (POST /api/google/search-chat/:siteId + gscAdmin.chat) — retire or re-home?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Retire the api wrapper; keep the server op until a deliberate server-side removal PR (public portal uses a separate route).

openQuestions.question: OQ6: AnomalyAlerts ownership — Search & Traffic (per ledger) or Home/Command Center (per HEAD mount at WorkspaceHome.tsx:614)?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Home surface owns it; record the hand-off so neither surface drops it.

openQuestions.question: OQ7: Confirm full date-preset sets (incl. 7d and 16mo GSC lookback) ship despite the prototype showing only 28d/90d/12m.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Ship full sets day one — presets are cheap and the reduction is a regression (O7/G3).

openQuestions.question: OQ8: Annotation campaign color — prototype uses purple, which is admin-AI-only under the Four Laws.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep HEAD palette (campaign=brand/teal); translate the prototype's ACAT map at port time.

openQuestions.question: OQ9: Homes for GSC devices/countries/search-types (Q9-Q11) and GA4 top pages/countries (G8/G11) — all endpoints exist but the prototype omits the cards.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: A 'Breakdowns' Drawer per tab (F3 Drawer) holding the omitted cards — preserves parity without cluttering the prototype layout.

openQuestions.question: Newly found: expose the annotation pageUrl field in the rebuilt create/edit form? The API + intelligence slices accept it (createAnnotationSchema, google.ts) but the admin form never sends it.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Yes — add an optional page-URL field; trivial cost, closes the form-less gap.

kitNewFeatures.rationale: Needs sn-search-traffic-2 (no cross-workspace GSC endpoint exists); deferring loses nothing at HEAD and it overlaps global-ops.

kitNewFeatures.rationale: New cross-surface write with no existing proof-point contract (sn-search-traffic-3); needs owner-signed contract with Insights Engine first.

composition.statesPlan: Per-source not-configured EmptyState (GSC and GA4 gate independently), Skeleton loading per tab, error banner with React Query Retry, needsSite nav-level locked state.
composition.mountNote: In-place behind ui-rebuild-shell flag with @ds-rebuilt markers (D2); AnnotatedTrendChart is carried-over-then-reskinned (T1) so click-to-annotate, line toggles, and callouts ship in the same phase — a static-chart quick win is a parity failure per the doc's own trade-off table.

crossSurfaceDeps: Insights Engine surface: OQ2 feed home + N3 proof-point contract
Home/cockpit surface: OQ6 AnomalyAlerts ownership hand-off (D5)
Diagnostics page: OQ3 Gap — I5 CTA deep-links to it (?report= param)
Keyword Hub: N2 must consume its rank read path (docs/rules/keyword-hub.md); N6 reads the strategy keyword-set endpoint
global-ops: N1 book roll-up overlaps its cross-workspace scope
client-portal: /api/public/analytics-annotations + ANNOTATION_BRIDGE_CREATED consumers must not break (A6/A7)
unknowns: Whether the Insights Engine / cockpit prototype actually re-homes the in-surface insight feed (I1-I5) — verifying requires reading other surfaces' docs, which was out of bounds; carried as OQ2 rather than assumed.
Whether the rebuilt shell reserves the '3' keyboard shortcut for this surface (S2) — global keymap for RebuiltAppChrome not defined in any file this audit was permitted to read.

verdicts.evidence: server/analytics-data.ts:27-33 (fetchPerformanceTrend already accepts dateRange? and forwards it); server/search-console.ts:730-734 (getPerformanceTrend signature includes dateRange?: CustomDateRange); server/search-console.ts:685-696 (gscDateRange honors a custom startDate/endDate window); server/search-console.ts:721-727 (getPreviousGscWindow already computes the prior-period window); server/routes/google.ts:258-270 (route reads only `days`, never threads dateRange).
verdicts.correction: existsToday=partial is directionally right but the evidence's rationale is wrong. The claim says you must 'extend fetchPerformanceTrend with an offset/startDate window' — but that helper ALREADY takes an optional dateRange (analytics-data.ts:27-33) that flows into getPerformanceTrend → gscDateRange, which already returns per-date rows for an arbitrary window (dimensions:['date']). getPreviousGscWindow (search-console.ts:721) already derives the prior window. The ONLY missing piece is route wiring: server/routes/google.ts:258-270 doesn't read startDate/endDate (or an offset) query params and pass a dateRange through. So this is a ~1-line route change plus a client that requests the prior window — no helper extension needed. Effort S stands, but scope is 'thread dateRange through the existing route', not 'extend the fetcher'. The 'comparison data already exists as a per-date series' claim remains correctly refuted: search-comparison returns only aggregate current/previous totals (shared/types/analytics.ts:47-52).

verdicts.evidence: server/routes/google.ts:245-322 (every GSC endpoint is per-site behind requireWorkspaceSiteAccessFromQuery + requireWorkspaceGscPropertyAccess, keyed to one siteId/gscSiteUrl); GSC fetchers (getSearchOverview/getPerformanceTrend et al.) are consumed only by analytics-data.ts, content-brief-generation-job.ts (single-workspace), and search-console.ts — no listWorkspaces() loop reads GSC to aggregate clicks/traffic/position across clients; server/workspaces.ts showPortfolioRollup is a per-workspace multi-location (locations WITHIN one client) rollup, not cross-client; server/mcp/tools/clients.ts cross-client tool aggregates pending approvals/requests/actions, not GSC metrics.
verdicts.correction: existsToday=no holds. Nearest-neighbor constructs (showPortfolioRollup, the clients.ts omit-workspaceId global summary) exist but neither aggregates GSC clicks/traffic/position across workspaces. Effort M is fair — new roll-up module + route, reads live GSC per site so no migration/mapper lockstep is required.

verdicts.evidence: grep for proof_point|proofPoint|proof-point across server/ shared/ src/ returns zero code hits; the only occurrences are shared/types/lexicon.ts:497 and :521, both wordClass:'proposed' with resolvingTicket:'reconcile-P2', explicitly describing mockup concepts (store.js / cockpit.js ck-tech 'graduate into the Insights Engine as proof points'). No graduation/promote-win write path exists.
verdicts.correction: existsToday=no holds; the wins→proof-point graduation bridge is unimplemented (mockup-only, proposed lexicon). Effort M is defensible but borderline: a persisted proof-point ledger row would pull in a migration + row mapper + serialization lockstep (per the DB column+mapper lockstep rule), which could push it to L. Flag as M/L, not clearly M.
